_comment: SI prefix table for the RESCUED parameter path ONLY (storeRawValue).
A value arriving under a column name no dictionary recognises used to be stored with its
number read but NEVER scaled — `"80m\u03a9@10V"` became 80 instead of 0.08, a thousand times
too high, in a slot the matching engine scores on (weight 9, `lte`). Measured over all 429
source files: 154,312 values were stored at the wrong scale, 50,521 of them in scoring slots,
across 69,742 products.
WHY AN ALLOWLIST RATHER THAN A RULE. A prefix rule that keys off the first letter is what
caused the damage it is supposed to fix: `startsWith('p')` reads `ppm` as pico. Measured over
the corpus, a first-letter rule mis-converts 11,254 `pcs`, 8,132 `ppm/`, 2,990 `ppm`, plus
`mil` / `Max` / `To` / `KE` / `KP`. But a strict whole-token rule is not the answer either --
it BREAKS 1,989 `PF`, 474 `nV/\u221aHz`, 431 `Mbit`, 391 `mW/sr`, 372 `Kbyte`, 262 `mW/\u00b0C`.
There are 367 distinct unit spellings in this corpus; recognising them all is its own project.
So this table is deliberately CONSERVATIVE and its safety property is one-directional:
a token that is not listed keeps the value EXACTLY as it is stored today. This change can
only make a number more correct or leave it untouched -- it can never introduce a wrong one.
An unrecognised unit is a missed opportunity, never a corruption.
SCOPE: the rescued path only. The dictionary-mapped paths already normalise and are NOT
touched -- applying this table there would REMOVE conversions that work today.
Shared verbatim by lib/services/atlasMapper.ts and scripts/atlas-ingest.mjs (the live ingest
path, which cannot import TS). One file, not two hand-copied lists -- see [[two-lists-will-drift]].
_prefixComment: GREEK MU (U+03BC) IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT and its absence is NOT an oversight.
extractNumericWithPrefix's unit character class is [a-zA-Z\u00b5\u03a9\u00b0%/\u221a] -- it
accepts the MICRO SIGN (U+00B5) but not GREEK SMALL LETTER MU (U+03BC). So a value written
with U+03BC yields NO parsed unit at all and can never reach this table; listing it here
would be dead code that reads like working code. (`applyUnitPrefix` already has a U+03BC
branch that is unreachable for exactly this reason -- a pre-existing gap, logged in BACKLOG.
Widening that regex would change the dictionary-MAPPED paths too, which is out of scope here.)
_excludedComment: Combinations the cross DOES produce but which are not prefixes:
MS  -- ambiguous (mega-siemens vs a mis-cased millisecond); never a real prefix here.
`mm` is NOT listed: `m` is not an atom, so the cross never generates it, and an exclusion
that cannot fire is a lie about the mechanism. Millimetre is safe because this is an
ALLOWLIST -- anything absent is left alone. A test pins that.
Uppercase P / U / N are deliberately ABSENT. A supplier writing `PF` for picofarad is real
(1,989 values), but enabling case-folding generatively also enables `UV`, `PW` and `NA` on
tokens that mean something else. That is a separate, measured change -- not this one.
